text.title: ELK / OpenSearch Centralized Logging Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Cluster bound to 0.0.0.0:9200 without auth; ransomware actor wipes indices, leaves a ransom note.

root-cause.source: Internal incident review, Elasticsearch security docs

antipattern.root-cause: Author followed an old 'just docker run' tutorial that disabled security.
antipattern.fix: Enable xpack.security; rotate built-in user passwords; restrict cluster network to private subnet + bastion; rebuild from backup.

antipattern.symptom: Single index grows to 8TB across 18 months; queries time out; cluster JVM OOMs.
antipattern.root-cause: Author created indices without rollover policy.
antipattern.fix: Define ILM policy: rollover at 50GB or 7 days; warm at 30d; cold at 90d; delete at 365d (per compliance). Apply to existing index via alias + reindex.

antipattern.symptom: Logstash grok pipeline takes 80% of cluster CPU parsing unstructured Nginx logs; ingest lag balloons.
antipattern.root-cause: Apps emit `[2026-05-23 INFO] ...` plain text; pipeline does regex extraction at ingest time.
antipattern.fix: Switch apps to structured JSON logs (e.g. pino / structlog); drop grok; ingest as-is into Elasticsearch.
